metadata.title:
VERY EXPENSIVE,


SAYS Pres. LOVET7

Will Take Up Union Depot Project
Hartman Chief.

pages.raw_text:
"A union depot in Spokane would
be a very expensive proposition,
and ! would not like to talk about
it until have conferred with Mr..
kruttschnitt."


This statement from the lips Of
Robert S. Lovett, the successor of
Hartman to the presidency of a
system controlling over 1900 miles
of railroad, is all that could be
CLEANED in regard to the proposed
union depot which Is aetating Spo-
kane TODAY.


Mr.. Lovett, in company with Vice
President J. p. O'Brien, General
Counsel w. w. Cotton, Traffic Man
ager j. G. Stubs, Director of Main
tenance and Operation j. krutt-
schnitt and seven other officials of
the Hartman system, composing
one of the most distinguished
groups of railroad officials who
ever visited Spokane, arrived in
Spokane last night from the east
and left at 1915 this morning for
Portland.


Il am very much pleased with
your city" stated President Lovett
this morning. It's air of enter
prise and seeming prosperity can
not but impress any visitor. | was
taken about the city this morning
and from the class of buildings 1t
